description: Pure yang energy, creative force, heaven
changingLines: Hidden dragon, do not act
Dragon appearing in the field
The superior man is active all day
Wavering flight over the depths
Flying dragon in the heavens
Arrogant dragon will have cause to repent

description: Pure yin energy, receptive force, earth
changingLines: Treading on hoarfrost, solid ice is not far off
Straight, square, great; without purpose, yet nothing remains unfurthered
Hidden lines; one is able to remain persevering
A tied-up sack; no blame, no praise
Yellow lower garment brings supreme good fortune
Dragons fight in the meadow; their blood is black and yellow

description: Initial difficulties, chaos before order, sprouting
changingLines: Hesitation and hindrance; it furthers one to remain persevering
Difficulties pile up; horse and wagon part
Whoever hunts deer without the forester only loses his way in the forest
Horse and wagon part; strive for union
Difficulties in blessing; a little perseverance brings good fortune
Horse and wagon part; bloody tears flow

description: Inexperience, the young fool, educating the immature
changingLines: To make a fool develop, it furthers one to apply discipline
To bear with fools in kindliness brings good fortune
Take not a maiden who sees a man of bronze and loses possession of herself
Entangled folly brings humiliation
Childlike folly brings good fortune
In punishing folly, it does not further one to commit transgressions
Waiting in the meadow; it furthers one to abide in what endures
Waiting on the sand; there is some gossip
Waiting in the mud brings about the arrival of the enemy
Waiting in blood; get out of the pit
Waiting at meat and drink; perseverance brings good fortune
One falls into the pit; three uninvited guests arrive
If one does not perpetuate the affair, there is a little gossip
One cannot engage in conflict; one returns home, gives way
To nourish oneself on ancient virtue induces perseverance
One cannot engage in conflict; turning back, one submits to fate
To contend before him brings supreme good fortune
Even if by chance a leather belt is bestowed on one, by the end of a morning it will have been snatched away three times

description: Collective force, discipline, leadership of the multitude
changingLines: An army must set forth in proper order
In the midst of the army; good fortune, no blame
Perchance the army carries corpses in the wagon; misfortune
The army retreats; no blame
There is game in the field; it furthers one to catch it
The great prince issues commands, founds states, vests families with fiefs
Hold to him in truth and loyalty; this is without blame
Hold to him inwardly; perseverance brings good fortune
You hold together with the wrong people
Hold to him outwardly also; perseverance brings good fortune
Manifestation of holding together; the king uses beaters on three sides only
He finds no head for holding together; misfortune

description: Gentle restraint, minor accumulation, the power of the small
changingLines: Return to the way; how could there be blame in this?
He allows himself to be drawn into returning; good fortune
The wagon is stripped of its spokes; man and wife roll their eyes
If you are sincere, blood vanishes and fear gives way
If you are sincere and loyally attached, you are rich in your neighbor
The rain comes, there is rest; honor is heaped up through perseverance

description: Conduct, treading carefully, walking on the tiger's tail
changingLines: Simple conduct; progress without blame
Treading a smooth, level course; perseverance of a dark man brings good fortune
A one-eyed man is able to see, a lame man is able to tread
He treads on the tail of the tiger; caution and circumspection lead ultimately to good fortune
Resolute conduct; perseverance with awareness of danger
Look to your conduct and weigh the favorable signs; supreme good fortune
When ribbon grass is pulled up, the sod comes with it
Bearing with the uncultured in gentleness, fording the river with resolution
No plain not followed by a slope, no going not followed by a return
He flutters down, not boasting of his wealth, together with his neighbor
The sovereign I gives his daughter in marriage; this brings blessing and supreme good fortune
The wall falls back into the moat; use no army now
When ribbon grass is pulled up, the sod comes with it
They bear and endure; this means good fortune for inferior people
They bear shame
He who acts at the command of the highest remains without blame
Standstill is giving way; good fortune for the great man
The standstill comes to an end; first standstill, then good fortune
Fellowship with men at the gate; no blame
Fellowship with men in the clan; humiliation
He hides weapons in the thicket; he climbs the high hill
He climbs up on his wall; he cannot attack
Men bound in fellowship first weep and lament, but afterward they laugh
Fellowship with men in the meadow; no remorse
No relationship with what is harmful; there is no blame in this
A big wagon for loading; one may undertake something
A prince offers it to the Son of Heaven; a petty man cannot do this
He makes a difference between himself and his neighbor; no blame
He whose truth is accessible, yet dignified, has good fortune
He is blessed by heaven; good fortune, nothing that does not further
A superior man modest about his modesty may cross the great water
Modesty that comes to expression; perseverance brings good fortune
A superior man of modesty and merit carries things to conclusion
Nothing that would not further; modesty in movement
No boasting of wealth before one's neighbor; it is favorable to attack with force
Modesty that comes to expression; it is favorable to set armies marching
Enthusiasm that expresses itself brings misfortune
Firm as a rock; not a whole day; perseverance brings good fortune
Enthusiasm that looks upward creates remorse; hesitation brings remorse
The source of enthusiasm; he achieves great things
Persistently ill, and still does not die
Deluded enthusiasm; but if after completion one changes, there is no blame

description: Adaptation, compliance, going with the flow
changingLines: The standard is changing; perseverance brings good fortune
If one clings to the little boy, one loses the strong man
If one clings to the strong man, one loses the little boy
Following creates success; perseverance brings misfortune
Sincere in the good; good fortune
He meets with firm allegiance and is still further bound

description: Correcting decay, renovation, fixing what has been spoiled
changingLines: Setting right what has been spoiled by the father
Setting right what has been spoiled by the mother
Setting right what has been spoiled by the father
Tolerating what has been spoiled by the father
Setting right what has been spoiled by the father
He does not serve kings and princes; he sets himself higher goals
Joint approach; perseverance brings good fortune
Joint approach; good fortune, everything furthers
Comfortable approach; nothing that would further
Complete approach; no blame
Wise approach; this is right for a great prince
Greathearted approach; good fortune, no blame

description: Observation, looking down, being an example
changingLines: Boylike contemplation; for an inferior man, no blame
Contemplation through the crack of the door; furthering for the perseverance of a woman
Contemplation of my life decides the choice between advance and retreat
Contemplation of the light of the kingdom; it furthers one to be the guest of a king
Contemplation of my life; the superior man is without blame
Contemplation of his life; the superior man is without blame
His feet are fastened in the stocks, so that his toes disappear
Bites through tender meat, so that his nose disappears
Bites on old dried meat and strikes on something poisonous
Bites on dried gristly meat; receives metal arrows
Bites on dried lean meat; receives yellow gold
His neck is fastened in the wooden cangue, so that his ears disappear
He lends grace to his toes, leaves the carriage, and walks
Lends grace to the beard on his chin
Graceful and moist; constant perseverance brings good fortune
Grace or simplicity? A white horse comes as if on wings
Grace in hills and gardens; the roll of silk is meager and small
Simple grace; no blame
The leg of the bed is split; those who persevere are destroyed
The bed is split at the edge; those who persevere are destroyed
He splits with them; no blame
The bed is split up to the skin; misfortune
A shoal of fishes; favor comes through the court ladies
There is a large fruit still uneaten; the superior man obtains a carriage

description: The turning point, renewal, coming back
changingLines: Return from a short distance; no need for remorse
Quiet return; good fortune
Repeated return; danger, no blame
Walking in the midst of others, one returns alone
Noblehearted return; no remorse
Missing the return; misfortune
Innocent behavior brings good fortune
If one does not count on the harvest while plowing
Undeserved misfortune; the cow that was tethered by someone
He who can be persevering remains without blame
Use no medicine in an illness incurred through no fault of your own
Innocent action brings misfortune; nothing furthers

description: Great accumulation, restraining force, potential stored
changingLines: Danger is at hand; it furthers one to desist
The wagon is stripped of its undercarriage
A good horse that follows others; awareness of danger
The headboard of a young bull; great good fortune
The tusk of a gelded boar; good fortune
One attains the way of heaven; success

description: Providing nourishment, seeking sustenance, the corners of the mouth
changingLines: You let your magic tortoise go and look at me with the corners of your mouth drooping
Turning to the summit for nourishment, deviating from the path
Turning away from nourishment; perseverance brings misfortune
Turning to the summit for provision of nourishment brings good fortune
Turning away from the path; to remain persevering brings good fortune
The source of nourishment; awareness of danger brings good fortune

description: Excess, preponderance of the great, critical mass
changingLines: To spread white rushes underneath; no blame
A dry poplar sprouts at the root; an older man takes a young wife
The ridgepole sags to the breaking point; misfortune
The ridgepole is braced; good fortune
A withered poplar puts forth flowers; an older woman takes a husband
One must go through the water; it goes over one's head
Repetition of the abyss; in the abyss one falls into a pit
The abyss is dangerous; one should strive to attain small things only
Forward and backward, abyss on abyss; in danger like this, pause at first
A jug of wine, a bowl of rice with it; earthen vessels
The abyss is not filled to overflowing; it is filled only to the rim
Bound with cords and ropes, shut in between thorn-hedged prison walls
Footprints run crisscross; if one is seriously intent, no blame
Yellow light; supreme good fortune
In the light of the setting sun, men either beat the pot and sing
Its coming is sudden; it flames up, dies down, is thrown away
In floods of tears, sighing and lamenting; good fortune
The king uses him to march forth and chastise; then it is best to kill the leaders
The influence shows itself in the big toe
The influence shows itself in the calves of the legs; misfortune
The influence shows itself in the thighs and holds to that which follows it
Perseverance brings good fortune; remorse disappears
The influence shows itself in the back of the neck; no remorse
The influence shows itself in the jaws, cheeks, and tongue
Seeking duration too hastily brings misfortune persistently
Remorse disappears
He who does not give duration to his character meets with disgrace
No game in the field
Giving duration to one's character through perseverance
Restlessness as an enduring condition brings misfortune
At the tail in retreat; this is dangerous
He holds him fast with yellow oxhide; no one can tear him loose
A halted retreat is nerve-wracking and dangerous
Voluntary retreat brings good fortune to the superior man
Friendly retreat; perseverance brings good fortune
Cheerful retreat; everything serves to further

description: Vigor, strength, the power of the great
changingLines: Power in the toes; continuing brings misfortune
Perseverance brings good fortune
The inferior man works through power; the superior man does not act thus
Perseverance brings good fortune; remorse disappears
Loses the goat with ease; no remorse
A goat butts against a hedge and gets its horns entangled
Progressing, but turned back; perseverance brings good fortune
Progressing, but in sorrow; perseverance brings good fortune
All are in accord; remorse disappears
Progress like a hamster; perseverance brings danger
Remorse disappears; take not gain and loss to heart
Making progress with the horns is permissible only for the purpose of punishing one's own city

description: Censorship, injury of the bright, eclipse
changingLines: Darkening of the light during flight; he lowers his wings
Darkening of the light injures him in the left thigh
Darkening of the light during the hunt in the south
He penetrates the left side of the belly
Darkening of the light as with Prince Chi; perseverance furthers
Not light but darkness; first he climbed up to heaven, then he plunged into the depths of the earth
Firm seclusion within the family; remorse disappears
She should not follow her whims; she must attend within to the food
When tempers flare up in the family, too great severity brings remorse
She is the treasure of the house; great good fortune
As a king he approaches his family; fear not
His work commands respect; in the end good fortune comes
Remorse disappears; if you lose your horse, do not run after it
One meets his lord in a narrow street; no blame
One sees the wagon dragged back, the oxen halted
Isolated through opposition, one meets a like-minded man
Remorse disappears; the companion bites his way through the wrappings
Isolated through opposition, one meets a like-minded man with whom one can associate in good faith
Going leads to obstructions, coming meets with praise
The king's servant is beset by obstruction upon obstruction
Going leads to obstructions; hence he comes back
Going leads to obstructions, coming leads to union
In the midst of the greatest obstructions, friends come
Going leads to obstructions, coming leads to great good fortune
Without blame
One kills three foxes in the field and receives a yellow arrow
If a man carries a burden on his back and nonetheless rides in a carriage
Deliver yourself from your great toe; then the companion comes
If only the superior man can deliver himself, it brings good fortune
The prince shoots at a hawk on a high wall; he kills it
Going quickly when one's tasks are finished is without blame
Perseverance furthers; to undertake something brings misfortune
When three people journey together, their number decreases by one
If a man decreases his faults, it makes the other hasten to come and rejoice
Someone does indeed increase him; ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose it
If one is increased without depriving others, there is no blame
It furthers one to accomplish great deeds; supreme good fortune, no blame
Someone does indeed increase him; ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose it
One is enriched through unfortunate events; no blame
If you walk in the middle and report to the prince, he will follow
If in truth you have a kind heart, ask not; supreme good fortune
He brings increase to no one; indeed, someone even strikes him
Mighty in the forward-striding toes; when one goes and is not equal to the task
A cry of alarm; arms at evening and at night
To be powerful in the cheekbones brings misfortune
There is no skin on his thighs, and walking comes hard
In dealing with weeds, firm resolution is necessary
No cry; in the end misfortune comes
It must be checked with a brake of bronze
There is a fish in the tank; no blame
There is no skin on his thighs, and walking comes hard
No fish in the tank; this leads to misfortune
A melon covered with willow leaves; hidden lines
He comes to meet with his horns; humiliation, no blame
If you are sincere, but not to the end, there will sometimes be confusion
Letting oneself be drawn brings good fortune and remains blameless
Gathering together amid sighs; nothing that would further
Great good fortune; no blame
If in gathering together one has position, this brings no blame
Lamenting and sighing, floods of tears; no blame
Pushing upward that meets with confidence brings great good fortune
If one is sincere, it furthers one to bring even a small offering
One pushes upward into an empty city
The king offers him Mount Chi; good fortune, no blame
Perseverance brings good fortune; one pushes upward by steps
Pushing upward in darkness; it furthers one to be unremittingly persevering
One sits oppressed under a bare tree and strays into a gloomy valley
One is oppressed while at meat and drink; the man with the scarlet knee bands is just coming
A man permits himself to be oppressed by stone and leans on thorns and thistles
He comes very quietly, oppressed in a golden carriage
His nose and feet are cut off; oppression at the hands of the man with the purple knee bands
He is oppressed by creeping vines; he moves uncertainly and says

description: The source, the water well, unchanging resource
changingLines: One does not drink the mud of the well; no animals come to an old well
At the wellhole one shoots fishes; the jug is broken and leaks
The well is cleaned, but no one drinks from it
The well is being lined; no blame
In the well there is a clear, cold spring from which one can drink
One draws from the well without hindrance; it is dependable
Wrapped in the hide of a yellow cow
When one's own day comes, one may create revolution
Starting brings misfortune; perseverance brings danger
Remorse disappears; men believe him
The great man changes like a tiger; even before he questions the oracle
The superior man changes like a panther; the inferior man molts in the face
A ting with legs upturned; furthers removal of stagnating stuff
There is food in the ting; my comrades are envious
The handle of the ting is altered; one is impeded in his way of life
The legs of the ting are broken; the prince's meal is spilled
The ting has yellow handles, golden carrying rings
The ting has rings of jade; great good fortune, nothing that would not act to further
Shock comes—oh, oh! Then follow laughing words—ha, ha!
Shock comes bringing danger; a hundred thousand times you lose your treasures
Shock comes and makes one distraught; if shock spurs to action, one remains free of misfortune
Shock is mired
Shock goes hither and thither; danger
Shock brings ruin and terrified gazing around; going ahead brings misfortune
Keeping his toes still; no blame
Keeping his calves still; he cannot rescue him whom he follows
Keeping his hips still; making his sacrum stiff
Keeping his trunk still; no blame
Keeping his jaws still; the words have order
Noblehearted keeping still; good fortune

description: Gradual progress, advancing step by step
changingLines: The wild goose gradually draws near the shore
The wild goose gradually draws near the cliff
The wild goose gradually draws near the plateau
The wild goose gradually draws near the tree
The wild goose gradually draws near the summit
The wild goose gradually draws near the clouds heights

description: Subordinate position, improper union, the younger sister
changingLines: The marrying maiden as a concubine; a lame man who is able to tread
A one-eyed man who is able to see; the perseverance of a solitary man furthers
The marrying maiden as a slave; she marries as a concubine
The marrying maiden draws out the allotted time
The sovereign I gave his daughter in marriage
The woman holds the basket, but there are no fruits in it
When a man meets his destined ruler, they can be together ten days
The curtain is of such fullness that the polestars can be seen at noon
The underbrush is of such abundance that the small stars can be seen at noon
The curtain is of such fullness that the polestars can be seen at noon
Lines are coming, blessing and fame draw near
His house is in a state of abundance; he screens off his family
If the wanderer busies himself with trivial things, he draws down misfortune upon himself
The wanderer comes to an inn; he has his property with him
The wanderer's inn burns down; he loses the steadfastness of his young servant
The wanderer rests in a shelter; he obtains his property and an ax
He shoots a pheasant; it drops with the first arrow
The bird's nest burns up; the wanderer first laughs, then must needs lament and weep
In advancing and in retreating, the perseverance of a warrior furthers
Penetration under the bed; priests and magicians are used in confusing number
Repeated penetration; humiliation
Remorse vanishes; during the hunt three kinds of game are caught
Perseverance brings good fortune; remorse vanishes, nothing that does not further
Penetration under the bed; he loses his property and his ax

description: Joy, the lake, pleasure and satisfaction
changingLines: Contented joyousness; good fortune
Sincere joyousness; good fortune, remorse disappears
Coming joyousness; misfortune
Joyousness that is weighed is not at peace
Sincerity toward disintegrating influences is dangerous
Seductive joyousness
He brings help with the strength of a horse; good fortune
At the dissolution he hurries to that which supports him; remorse disappears
He dissolves his self; no remorse
He dissolves his bond with his group; supreme good fortune
His loud cries are as dissolving as sweat; dissolution!
He dissolves his blood; departing, keeping at a distance, going out, is without blame
Not going out of the gate and the courtyard is without blame
Not going out of the gate and the courtyard brings misfortune
He who knows no limitation will have cause to lament
Contented limitation; success
Sweet limitation brings good fortune; going brings esteem
Galling limitation; perseverance brings misfortune
Being prepared brings good fortune; if there are secret designs, it is disquieting
A crane calling in the shade; its young answers it
He finds a comrade; now he beats the drum, now he stops
The moon nearly at the full; the team horse goes astray
He possesses truth, which links together; no blame
Cockcrow penetrating to heaven; perseverance brings misfortune

description: Preponderance of the small, slight excess
changingLines: The bird meets with misfortune through flying
She passes by her ancestor and meets her ancestress
If one is not extremely careful, somebody may come up from behind and strike him
No blame; he meets him without passing by
Dense clouds, no rain from our western region; the prince shoots and hits him who is in the cave
He passes him by, not meeting him; the flying bird leaves him

description: Order achieved, completion, the crossing is completed
changingLines: He brakes his wheels; gets his tail in the water
The woman loses the curtain of her carriage
The Illustrious Ancestor disciplines the Devil's Country
The finest clothes turn to rags; be careful all day long
The neighbor in the east who slaughters an ox does not attain as much real happiness
He gets his head in the water; danger

description: Not yet complete, transition, before the crossing
changingLines: He gets his tail in the water; humiliating
He brakes his wheels; perseverance brings good fortune
Before completion, attack brings misfortune
Perseverance brings good fortune, remorse disappears
Perseverance brings good fortune, no remorse
There is drinking of wine in genuine confidence; no blame
